title: S-Det deterministic-choice contract
description: Specifies that a map, assignment, representative, payer, label, route, or tie-break is a total reproducible function of recorded branch-state data. The record operationalizes the S-Det deterministic-choice lemma schema in the structural exhaustion framework (branch_closure_methodology_extended.tex, "Lemma schemas").

schema.description: Required contract discriminator; it must be the literal `S-Det`.

choiceFunction.description: Required mathematical definition of the map or rule that selects the object, representative, payer, label, support, assignment, payload route, worksheet row, finite-table representative, or certificate handle.

informal.description: Required nonempty prose specification of the function's domain, codomain, inputs, and selected output.

latex.description: Optional LaTeX rendering of the choice function.

formalLanguage.description: Optional name and version of the formal language used to encode the function.

formal.description: Optional machine-checkable definition of the same function.

recordedDataDependencies.description: Required nonempty list of every branch-state datum read by the choice function. Dependencies must be fixed by H_0, order, E, I, R, V, Q, A, declared labels, ledgers, budgets, or prior canonical constructions; unrecorded search outcomes or arbitrary choices are not permitted.

items.description: One precise state-field path, symbol, order, or previously certified canonical object on which the choice depends.

totalityStatement.description: Required statement proving that the choice function returns an allowed value for every state in its declared domain, including degenerate cases such as an empty family when those are possible.

informal.description: Required nonempty prose totality claim with the function's domain and existence conditions.

formalLanguage.description: Optional name and version of the formal language used for the totality theorem.

formal.description: Optional machine-checkable totality theorem for the choice function.

tieBreakRule.description: Optional explicit rule resolving every non-unique optimum, representative, payer, label, finite-table row, route, or assignment by a recorded total order. It is required in content whenever downstream reasoning treats a non-unique construction as canonical.

canonicalityUse.description: Optional explanation of where downstream reasoning relies on this deterministic choice, such as ledger assignment pi, payer selection, label choice, payload construction, finite-table enumeration, comparison of selected objects, or reproducible audit records.

evidence.description: Required nonempty list of sources proving the function is well defined, total, and canonical from the recorded dependencies.

items.description: One evidence record supporting determinism, totality, or the stated canonicality use.

kind.description: Required evidence category from the fixed evidence-kind alphabet.

reference.description: Required nonempty locator, preferably the constructor definition, S-Def/S-Det record, tie-break lemma, ledger-assignment proof, routing-table proof, or formal implementation.

hash.description: Optional integrity hash for the implementation, table, or formal artifact that realizes the choice.

notes.description: Optional note explaining how the evidence covers all inputs and resolves non-uniqueness.
